one_line_takeaway: SACA turns failed navigation rollouts into dense step-level training signal via a training-free foundation-model auditor plus scenario-routed group construction, delivering new SOTA on R2R-CE and RxR-CE from single-RGB input -- a sample-efficiency/RL paper, not a security one.
key_finding: SACA sets a new state of the art on both VLN-CE benchmarks using only single ego-centric RGB. With no extra training data it reaches 60.3% SR / 55.1% SPL on R2R-CE and 60.3% SR / 49.8% SPL on RxR-CE, beating the prior best single-RGB method StreamVLN by +7.5 SR / +7.9 SPL on R2R and by +11.7 SR / +7.3 SPL on the longer-horizon RxR. It even surpasses ETPNav, which uses panoramic + odometry + depth sensors, by +3.3 (R2R) and +5.6 (RxR) SR. Ablation (Table 2) isolates each contribution's value on RxR SR: SFT baseline 48.6 -> GRPO 49.9 -> +Soft Score 51.8 -> +All-Failure Rescue 56.4 -> +Repair Resampling (full SACA) 60.3, showing All-Failure Rescue is the single largest jump (+4.6). The motivating observation is quantified: ~73% of failed episodes still correctly execute their initial sub-instructions, i.e. contain reusable valid prefixes that standard RL discards.
core_contribution: The paper proposes Step-Aware Contrastive Alignment (SACA), a reinforcement fine-tuning (RFT) framework for Vision-Language Navigation in Continuous Environments (VLN-CE) that extracts dense, step-level supervision from failed/imperfect trajectories, avoiding both the compounding errors of pure SFT and the sparse-reward gradient collapse of standard GRPO. Three concrete components form the contribution: (1) a Perception-Grounded Step-Aware (PGSA) auditor that uses frozen zero-shot foundation models (a tiny Qwen3-0.6B LLM to parse instructions into landmarks, plus GroundingDINO, SAM3, and CLIP) to produce a continuous 'Soft Score' for trajectory ranking and a discrete 'Hard Mask' that pinpoints the exact 'Divergence Point' where an agent drifts off-course -- with no domain-specific/trained Process Reward Model; (2) a Scenario-Conditioned Group Construction mechanism that routes each sampled group of trajectories to 'Repair Resampling' when the group contains at least one success (mixed-outcome) or to 'All-Failure Rescue' when every trajectory fails (null-outcome); and (3) a robust SACA optimization objective combining trajectory-level advantages with two step-level constraints applied only to the best failure ('Pseudo-Anchor') -- Consistency Alignment (behavior cloning on the valid prefix) and Contrastive Correction (penalizing the divergence-point action against a shortest-path teacher action).
threat_model: Not applicable. This is not a security paper. The knowledge-graph cluster label 'Reward Hacking / RLHF' is misleading here: 'reward hacking' appears only once, as a one-line motivation for NOT training a domain-specific Process Reward Model (PRMs are 'costly and prone to reward hacking'). SACA sidesteps that by using frozen zero-shot perception models as the auditor. There is no adversary, no attack surface, and no defense being evaluated; the work is about sample efficiency and error recovery in embodied navigation RL. No threat model was invented.
limitations: (1) The PGSA auditor's reliability rests entirely on frozen open-vocabulary perception models (GroundingDINO/SAM3/CLIP); the paper does not report auditor accuracy or how detection/segmentation errors propagate into wrong divergence points. (2) There is an internal inconsistency in the reported numbers: the prose (Sec 4.2, 'Scaling with Additional Data') states SACA-dagger reaches 62.7% SR on R2R-CE, but Table 1 lists the SACA-dagger R2R-CE SR as 64.7% -- so the with-extra-data R2R figure is ambiguous. (3) Only one policy backbone (LLaVA-Video-8B) and two benchmarks are tested; no real-robot or sim-to-real evaluation. (4) The method adds substantial machinery (multiple foundation models in the loop, resampling up to Nrep times, several thresholds tau_det/tau_s/tau_h and weights w1-w3/lambda/kappa/s) whose per-iteration compute/latency cost versus plain GRPO is not quantified. (5) The contrastive correction relies on a simulator shortest-path teacher action, a privileged signal available only in simulation. (6) Only val-unseen results are shown; no test-server / leaderboard numbers.

datasets_used: ScaleVLN dataset (used only for the dagger/extra-data variant)
quantitative_results: SACA (no extra data), R2R-CE Val-Unseen: NE 4.57, OS 64.9, SR 60.3%, SPL 55.1%
SACA (no extra data), RxR-CE Val-Unseen: NE 4.90, SR 60.3%, SPL 49.8%, nDTW 62.1
Improvement over StreamVLN on R2R-CE: +7.5% SR, +7.9% SPL; on RxR-CE: +11.7% SR, +7.3% SPL

SACA-dagger (with ScaleVLN extra data), Table 1: R2R-CE NE 4.19, OS 69.3, SR 64.7%, SPL 56.9; RxR-CE NE 4.75, SR 62.1%, SPL 51.7%, nDTW 66.0 (note: prose reports 62.7% R2R SR, conflicting with the table's 64.7%)
Surpasses ETPNav (panoramic+odometry+depth) by +3.3% (R2R) and +5.6% (RxR) SR despite using only single RGB
Component ablation (Table 2, RxR-CE SR): SFT baseline 48.6% -> GRPO 49.9% -> +Soft Score 51.8% -> +All-Failure Rescue 56.4% -> +Repair Resampling (full) 60.3%
Perception-module ablation (Table 5, R2R-CE SR): CLIP-only 57.6% -> +GroundingDINO 59.2% -> +SAM3 masks 60.3%
Objective ablation (full 60.3% R2R SR / 55.1% SPL): removing Consistency Align -> 58.0% SR / 51.6% SPL; removing Contrastive Correction -> 57.3% SR (RxR SR drops to 56.4%)
Hyperparameter ablation: group size K=8 optimal (K=4 -> RxR SR 55.2%, K=16 -> R2R SR 60.1%); max repairs Nrep=3 optimal (Nrep=1 -> R2R SR 58.1%, Nrep=5 -> R2R SR 60.0%)
~73% of failed episodes successfully execute the initial sub-instructions (reusable valid prefixes)
baselines_compared: StreamVLN (primary single-RGB SOTA baseline; also the SFT baseline in Table 2)
VLN-R1 (first to apply GRPO in VLN-CE)
InstructNav, AG-CMTP, R2R-CMTP, LAW, CM2, WS-MGMap, ETPNav+FF
Standard GRPO (RFT ablation baseline within SACA)
